meaning: n.霹雳,雷电,意外的灾难,突发事件
examples: This was a thunderbolt out of a clear sky.
The news was like a thunderbolt out of a clear sky.
Martine Aubry, the Socialist Party leader, called it a “thunderbolt”.
Apple updated the already-well-established product with more advanced Intel Core processors, Thunderbolt, and Lion.
Bryson, who is 55, hints at where the boomer mind-set is going in Thunderbolt Kid.
